paper_title: The largest subgraph without a forbidden induced subgraph
summary: Conjecture 6.1 from arXiv:2405.05902, asserting that ex(Gamma, P_{C_{2l}}) <= C t^{1-1/l} n^{1+1/l} for every (c,t)-sparse graph Gamma, was resolved by Ding, Gao, Liu, Luan, and Sun in November 2024. Their paper arXiv:2411.12659 proves that if an n-vertex (c,t)-sparse graph has at least C t^{1-1/l} n^{1+1/l} edges then it must contain an induced copy of C_{2l}, establishing the conjectured bound.

since_posted.title: Induced even cycles in locally sparse graphs
since_posted.authors: Laihao Ding, Jun Gao, Hong Liu, Bingyu Luan, Shumin Sun
since_posted.claim: Proves that every (c,t)-sparse n-vertex graph with at least C t^{1-1/l} n^{1+1/l} edges contains an induced copy of C_{2l}, thereby resolving the conjecture of Fox, Nenadov, and Pham.

search_queries: induced Turán number even cycles sparse graph Fox Nenadov Pham 2405.05902
ex(Gamma, P_{C_{2l}}) sparse graph "induced Turán" even cycle clique quotient
notes: arXiv:2411.12659 (submitted 2024-11-19) explicitly states in its abstract that it resolves the conjecture of Fox, Nenadov and Pham, with the bound matching Conjecture 6.1 exactly.
